note: Tier-K motion graphics rendered in Remotion + FFmpeg. Exact text, dates, claim-linked citations, counters, diagrams, captions and labels are code-rendered, not embedded in generated images.

global.citation_policy: Use citation lower-thirds for case/vote/standard graphics tied to claim_id.

reusable_components: SymbolicReconstructionLabel: small persistent label used on AI reenactment/location sequences.
LegalScale: hunch -> reasonable suspicion -> probable cause -> warrant, with animated threshold markers.
FourthAmendmentWall: dark wall with a narrow electric-blue gap.
PathCounter: top-down sidewalk/store-window path animation with counter ticking to about 12.
WeaponsOnlyFrisk: outer-clothing boundary icon and no-pocket-search warning.
VoteCard: 8-1 majority graphic with one dissent dot.
CitationLowerThird: case citation and claim-linked note.
ModernMontageCards: fact tiles for place/action/pattern, assembling into reasonable suspicion.
PhoneBridge: phone glow in pocket for EP7 tease.
OpenCaption: later force-aligned captions, bottom third.

key_graphics.spec: Small lower-left label appears over the POV stop sequence, then fades before kinetic hook type.
Wall appears solid, then a narrow gap opens with electric-blue light. Caption: Suspicion, not proof.
Two anonymous path lines alternate past a storefront. Counter ticks to ~12, with smaller note five or six each implied visually by alternating passes.
Probable cause + warrant sit high on the scale; suspicion marker remains below it.
1968 timeline hit, 8-1 vote, lower-third Terry v. Ohio, 392 U.S. 1.
Scale labels: hunch, reasonable suspicion, probable cause. Reasonable suspicion lights up below probable cause.
Fact tiles appear: two men, same window, about 12 trips, heads together. Tiles assemble into specific and articulable facts.
Outer clothing boundary highlights, pockets stay locked/dim. Caption: Outer clothing - weapons only.
Stop = seizure; Frisk = search; limited + reasonable. Three-beat type build.
Eight majority dots dim; one gold dissent dot remains with Douglas, J. (alone).
Training sharpens judgment on one side; bias bends judgment on the other. No group-specific claim.
Street/pocket motif becomes a glowing phone silhouette. Caption: Next: can they search your phone?
